response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with broad exposure across web, mobile, and distributed systems, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its primary requirement and he has only a brief 6-month C++ engagement at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — both from over a decade ago — with C++ not even appearing in his listed skills. The job also requires industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT/OPC UA protocol experience, and IoT systems work, none of which are present in the resume. His strengths in event-driven architecture, high-throughput services, and agile practices are genuinely relevant but insufficient to offset the absence of the core competencies.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and concurrent patterns
Consistent event-driven and message-broker architecture work across multiple roles, directly mapping to the messaging and scalability requirements of the role
Long track record of agile practices including TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing, and mentoring others through workshops and code katas
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — one 6-month role at National Instruments and a thesis project, with no C++ listed among current skills
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850, or any related industrial protocol experience
No knowledge of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or any industrial cybersecurity standards mentioned anywhere in the resume
